all_violations.description: Regex assertions are coupled to YAML whitespace/newline formatting, so semantically equivalent formatting changes can trigger false failures.
all_violations.suggestion: Parse workflow YAML and assert structured fields instead of whitespace-sensitive regex fragments.

all_violations.description: Tests assert directly against mutable repository workflow files, so unrelated concurrent edits can affect outcomes.
all_violations.suggestion: Snapshot required workflow fixtures into isolated temp workspaces per test when validating parsing logic.

all_violations.description: Spec file size (225 lines) increases cognitive load and makes failures harder to localize quickly.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by acceptance criterion into smaller focused files with shared helper imports.

all_violations.description: Spec file size (180 lines) and broad scope reduce readability and maintenance speed.
all_violations.suggestion: Partition into policy-graph, report-summary, and local-diagnostics focused specs.

all_violations.description: Workflow parsing helpers are duplicated across multiple specs, increasing drift risk.
all_violations.suggestion: Extract escapeRegex/getJobBlock/getNeeds into a shared helper under tests/support/utils.

all_violations.description: Repeated helper implementation duplicates parsing semantics and adds maintenance overhead.
all_violations.suggestion: Import shared parser helpers to keep behavior consistent.

all_violations.description: Large boolean conjunctions inside single expects obscure which precondition failed.
all_violations.suggestion: Split assertions into named expect statements for faster failure diagnosis.

all_violations.description: Legacy [P0]/[P1] IDs reduce traceability consistency with story-scoped structured IDs used elsewhere.
all_violations.suggestion: Adopt structured IDs (e.g., E6-ATDD-E2E-XXX) for this suite.
all_violations.code_snippet: test('[P0] defines required policy-first quality-stage jobs ...')

all_violations.description: Coverage is heavily skewed toward static regex checks of workflow/config text, with limited runtime execution-path validation.
all_violations.suggestion: Add at least one execution-oriented CI contract test that evaluates job graph behavior from resolved workflow data.
all_violations.code_snippet: test('[P0] defines required policy-first quality-stage jobs ...')

all_violations.description: Current tests strongly validate blocker conditions but do not explicitly simulate a full all-green merge-ready path for all required gates together.
all_violations.suggestion: Add a happy-path release-readiness test that verifies policy/lint/test/burn-in/quality-gates complete and status resolves ready.

all_violations.description: Operator journey checks are primarily script/doc assertions rather than end-user UI interaction flows.
all_violations.suggestion: Introduce one targeted UI flow assertion for rollout control visibility/diagnostics where applicable.
all_violations.code_snippet: test('[E6-AUTOMATE-E2E-203][P1] scheduled burn-in workflow keeps docs-only PR skip behavior ...')

all_violations.description: Large workflow/script files are repeatedly read in multiple tests, adding avoidable per-test IO overhead.
all_violations.suggestion: Cache parsed workflow/script content in beforeAll or fixture setup and share immutable snapshots.

all_violations.description: Multiple policy harness invocations are executed serially inside single tests, increasing runtime under constrained CI workers.
all_violations.suggestion: Use table-driven or batched harness execution where deterministic and safe, or split into smaller focused tests.

high_severity_violations.description: Spec file size (225 lines) increases cognitive load and makes failures harder to localize quickly.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by acceptance criterion into smaller focused files with shared helper imports.

high_severity_violations.description: Spec file size (180 lines) and broad scope reduce readability and maintenance speed.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Partition into policy-graph, report-summary, and local-diagnostics focused specs.

high_severity_violations.description: Coverage is heavily skewed toward static regex checks of workflow/config text, with limited runtime execution-path validation.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Add at least one execution-oriented CI contract test that evaluates job graph behavior from resolved workflow data.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: test('[P0] defines required policy-first quality-stage jobs ...')

top_10_recommendations.recommendation: Keep avoiding hard waits and random/time primitives in assertions.
Prefer parser-based assertions for workflow semantics over formatting-sensitive regex checks.
Continue using deterministic fixture/harness setup for policy checks.
Continue using temp-repo harnesses for mutation-style policy tests.
Isolate static workflow reads in fixture snapshots to reduce coupling to repo churn.
Keep test cases parallel-safe by avoiding shared mutable runtime state.
Split large spec files by acceptance criterion or behavior axis.
Extract duplicated workflow-parsing helpers into shared utilities.
Replace conjunction-style asserts with granular, named expects.
Standardize test ID schema across all CI policy specs.
